subtasks.description: Query sam_licenses to extract current Adobe contract state: product lines, quantities, unit prices, term dates, and total contracted spend.
Query consumption_facts for Q4 2024 actual spend by Adobe product line and 90-day usage trend (current consumption rate vs. contracted capacity).

Reconcile sam_licenses contract state with consumption_facts spend and usage data. Classify each Adobe product line as expand (usage >90% of contract, positive trend), hold (usage 50-90%, stable), or reduce (usage <50%, declining). Generate renewal recommendations with trajectory rationale.

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks decompose the analysis into independently parallelizable data collection (adobe_contracts as small since it's a single-table lookup of contract terms; adobe_spend_usage as mid because it requires aggregation and trend calculation across Q4 spend and 90-day rolling window), then a frontier cross_recon that synthesizes both data sources into a multi-signal classification framework (expand/hold/reduce posture per product line with trajectory reasoning). The frontier tier is justified for cross_recon because it must correlate usage trends with contract positions, handle potential anomalies in spend-to-usage ratios, and generate strategic renewal recommendations—tasks requiring inference and multi-signal reasoning. This plan avoids unnecessary splits: both SQL queries run in parallel, and the reconciliation step includes classification and recommendation generation rather than adding downstream policy or planning subtasks (those are out of scope per the query intent).

output.sql: SELECT c.product, c.daily_active_seats as quantity, CASE WHEN c.amount_usd > 0 AND c.daily_active_seats > 0 THEN c.amount_usd / c.daily_active_seats ELSE 0 END as unit_price, c.event_date as contract_start_date, c.fiscal_year, c.fiscal_quarter, SUM(c.amount_usd) as total_contracted_spend, AVG(c.daily_active_seats) as avg_active_seats FROM consumption_facts c WHERE c.publisher = 'Adobe' AND c.event_date >= '2024-10-01' AND c.event_date <= '2024-12-31' AND c.fiscal_quarter = 4 AND c.fiscal_year = 2024 GROUP BY c.product, c.daily_active_seats, c.amount_usd, c.event_date, c.fiscal_year, c.fiscal_quarter ORDER BY total_contracted_spend DESC

output.raw_output:
{"sql": "SELECT
    product,
    SUM(amount_usd) AS q4_2024_actual_spend,
    AVG(daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats_q4,
    MAX(daily_active_seats) AS peak_daily_active_seats_q4,
    MIN(daily_active_seats) AS min_daily_active_seats_q4,
    COUNT(DISTINCT event_date) AS active_days_q4,
    -- 90-day usage trend: compare last 30 days vs prior 60 days avg daily active seats
    AVG(CASE WHEN event_date >= '2024-12-01' THEN daily_active_seats END) AS avg_seats_last_30_days,
    AVG(CASE WHEN event_date >= '2024-10-01' AND event_date < '2024-12-01' THEN daily_active_seats END) AS avg_seats_prior_60_days,
    -- Trend ratio: >1 means growing usage, <1 means declining
    ROUND(
        AVG(CASE WHEN event_date >= '2024-12-01' THEN daily_active_seats END)
        / NULLIF(AVG(CASE WHEN event_date >= '2024-10-01' AND event_date < '2024-12-01' THEN daily_active_seats END), 0),
    4) AS usage_trend_ratio,
    -- Recommendation signal based on trend
    CASE
        WHEN ROUND(
            AVG(CASE WHEN event_date >= '2024-12-01' THEN daily_active_seats END)
            / NULLIF(AVG(CASE WHEN event_date >= '2024-10-01' AND event_date < '2024-12-01' THEN daily_active_seats END), 0),
        4) > 1.10 THEN 'EXPAND'
        WHEN ROUND(
            AVG(CASE WHEN event_date >= '2024-12-01' THEN daily_active_seats END)
            / NULLIF(AVG(CASE WHEN event_date >= '2024-10-01' AND event_date < '2024-12-01' THEN daily_active_seats END), 0),
        4) BETWEEN 0.90 AND 1.10 THEN 'HOLD'
        WHEN ROUND(
            AVG(CASE WHEN event_date >= '2024-12-01' THEN daily_active_seats END)
            / NULLIF(AVG(CASE WHEN event_date >= '2024-10-01' AND event_date < '2024-12-01' THEN daily_active_seats END), 0),
        4) < 0.90 THEN 'REDUCE'
        ELSE 'INSUFFICIENT DATA'
    END AS renewal_recommendation,
    SUM(CASE WHEN event_date >= '2024-12-01' THEN amount_usd END) AS spend_last_30_days,
    SUM(CASE WHEN event_date >= '2024-10-01' AND event_date < '2024-12-01' THEN amount

output.note: Upstream outputs did not contain resolved MongoDB sam_licenses entities or completed SQL aggregation results; reconciliation could not be performed with available data.
